# Preface {.unnumbered}

This training introduces energy system modeling in R with the
[energyRt](https://github.com/optimal2050/energyRt) package — energy system
modeling with a familiar R workflow: you describe the system as R objects,
energyRt compiles and solves the optimization model on one of several
mathematical-programming **backends** (GLPK, Julia/JuMP, Python/Pyomo, or
GAMS), and results come back as tidy tables.

The first chapter, [Installation](installation.qmd), walks through installing R,
the energyRt package, and at least one solver backend so your machine is ready
for the hands-on sessions.

<!-- The rest of the tutorial is hands-on, in two parts. **Build UTOPIA** -->
<!-- (@sec-first-model – @sec-utopia): solve a first tiny model end to end, then -->
<!-- grow the classic UTOPIA electricity model brick by brick — commodities, supply -->
<!-- and demand, technologies in depth (with levelized costs and reports), time -->
<!-- slices, weather and storage — until it assembles into the exact model that -->
<!-- ships with energyRt as the `utopia_modules` kit. **Use UTOPIA** -->
<!-- (@sec-scenarios): run policy scenarios (CO~2~ cap, carbon tax, renewable -->
<!-- share, nuclear moratorium) and compare them. Each chapter ends with exercises; -->
<!-- worked solutions are folded underneath. -->

<!-- The [intro slides](slides/intro.html) give the 10-minute framing (what an -->
<!-- energy-system model is, and how the day is structured); the mechanics live here -->
<!-- in the tutorial. -->

::: {.callout-tip}
The installation chapter shows every command but does **not** run any of them at
render time — copy each command into your own R session to execute it.
:::
